Table of Contents

Interface IRasterCatalogTable

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster
Assembly
ESRI.Server.DatasourcesRaster.dll

Provides access to members that control a raster catalog table.

[Guid("AC0E9740-0742-11D4-8D75-00C04F5B87B2")]
[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
public interface IRasterCatalogTable

Properties

FieldNameLocation

Location of the field name of the raster catalog.

int FieldNameLocation { get; }

Property Value

int

OID of the ith raster in the raster catalog.

int OID[int idx] { get; }

Parameters

idx int

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

RasterDataset[int]

Reference to the ith raster dataset in the raster catalog.

IRasterDataset RasterDataset[int idx] { get; }

Parameters

idx int

Property Value

IRasterDataset

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>The <STRONG>RasterDataset</STRONG> object represents a dataset on disk or in a database. It is composed of one or more persistent raster bands. The dataset provides access only to operations that apply to the entire dataset, with the <STRONG>RasterBand</STRONG> providing access to operations that occur on individual bands.</P> <P>The RasterDataset object performs basic dataset management functions, such as copy, rename, and delete. It can also instantiat RasterBand and <STRONG>Raster</STRONG> objects representing other aspects of the data. It can be used to examine dataset properties including different format, extent, spatial reference, and number of bands.</P> <P>The spatial reference of the raster raster dataset can be changed through interface <STRONG>IGeoDatasetSchemaEdit</STRONG>, but it does not project the dataset, only changes the coordinate system associated with the dataset.<BR>The pyramid of the raster dataset can be built or rebuilt to improve display performance for large raster datasets.</P>

RasterExtent[int]

Extent of the ith raster in the catalog.

idx int

IEnvelope
